tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Strait of Hormuz effectively closed post-Feb 28 US-Israeli strikes on Iran. Brent surged 13% to peak; now stabilising at $79.40/bbl (Day 4) with sustained geopolitical risk premium. Goldman Sachs models $18/bbl 'Iran shock' add-on; BloombergNEF targets $91/bbl under prolonged disruption. ASEAN energy producers (PTTEP, Medco Energi, Sapura Energy) benefit directly from price spike. 23% of global LNG trade transits Hormuz — Qatar's 77 mtpa effectively offline. Long near-dated Brent vs short deferred as backwardation steepens. Entry at $79; base target $91, stress target $110. Saudi/UAE spare capacity ramp (3 weeks) is the key near-term downside catalyst to monitor.

tradeIdeas.title: Long ASEAN Banks / Short ASEAN Energy Equities
tradeIdeas.rationale: ASEAN Banks index at 216.4 (-1.0% on Mar 3) — selloff overdone for net-oil-exporting names benefiting from MYR/THB carry. ASEAN Energy Equities at 135.8 (+1.7%) bid on oil spike; prior thesis of falling-oil short now reversed. Recommend unwinding energy equity short leg and rotating into bank long for rate-cut delay beneficiaries. iTraxx Asia IG at 132bp (+4bp) signals credit still widening but stabilising vs prior-day 26bp spike. MUFG flags MYR and SGD bank balance sheets as relatively insulated vs PHP/IDR. Wilmar and IJM plantation equity longs complement as oil-correlated ASEAN plays.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short — Elevated Conviction on Iran Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: PHP most vulnerable ASEAN currency per MUFG (Michael Wan, Mar 3). USD/PHP at 58.35 with DOE warning of immediate fuel price increases (~₱2/liter). >50% of Philippines power generation relies on imported fossil fuels; no strategic LNG reserves. Philippines current account deficit widens to -4.5% of GDP at $90/bbl. BSP rate cut cycle now on hold indefinitely — but IDR and THB also pressured, narrowing yield support. MUFG stress target: 54.50 on sustained oil spike implies near-term risk to 59–60 range. JKM LNG at $15.60 and rising compounds LNG import cost exposure. Stop-loss at 57.50.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short — Dual Exposure to Iran Shock and Shadow Fleet Risk
tradeIdeas.rationale: IDR at 16,987 (Day 4 of Iran shock, +0.5% on Mar 3) and deteriorating. Net oil importer exposed to Hormuz-driven price surge; Bank Indonesia forced into defensive rate hold at 4.75% despite growth pressure. Indonesia flagged by OFAC and UN for Iranian crude transshipment via Karimun port (18,242 bpd); MAS emergency guidance (Mar 3) amplifies regional sanctions scrutiny. Indonesian-branded crude surging into China (~291,000 bpd) covering Iranian origin — OFAC enforcement tightening. Target 17,500–18,000 in stress. Brent at $79.40 baseline; stress target $100 accelerates IDR trajectory.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection — Hold Position
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG at 132bp (+4bp on Mar 3, stabilising after +26bp spike on Mar 2). Iran shock + simultaneous SCS Heightened risk creates dual-front credit deterioration. CDS entered at 102bp on pre-shock level — now deep in-the-money. Day 4 stabilisation suggests near-term tactical pause, not reversal: Oman back-channel talks rejected, Hormuz mines confirmed. Hold existing 5Y position; 180–220bp is credible stress target in 1–3 month Hormuz disruption scenario. Sapura Energy and Hyflux bonds are the most vulnerable ASEAN HY names. ASEAN HY Composite at 432bp (+7bp) still widening.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF — Spread Re-widening on Hormuz Shock
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM at $15.60/MMBtu (+5.4% on Mar 3) as Asian buyers scramble with Qatar Hormuz supply offline. TTF at €34.2/MWh (+2.1%) rising but less acutely — Atlantic Basin LNG supply available as substitution. 84% of Hormuz LNG goes to Asia; TTF can diversify sourcing. JKM/TTF spread widening on Day 4 confirms Asian supply squeeze is structural under Hormuz closure. Wilmar (palm oil / biodiesel competitor to LNG) and PTTEP (LNG producer beneficiary) are key client-linked expressions of this theme. In 1-month Hormuz closure, JKM could approach $25/MMBtu. Long JKM outright or long JKM vs short TTF captures asymmetric Asian supply squeeze.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: CRITICAL: Sapura Energy's MYR debt maturities 2026–2027 represent an acute refinancing risk under current market conditions. iTraxx Asia IG at 132bp and ASEAN HY at 432bp signal the window is still open for secured bank refinancing but narrowing rapidly. E&P capex clients recovering above $79/bbl Brent threshold, but capex lag means Sapura revenue uplift is 2–3 quarters behind oil price. High leverage (net debt ~MYR 10bn) and MAS Iran sanctions guidance (Mar 3) create compounding risk — bilateral bank discussions must commence immediately before market further tightens.

THB bond maturity H2 2026 — BOT rate hold (inflation pressure from Brent at $79.40) means pre-financing at current 3.10% TH 10Y is the optimal window. Iran shock creates oil revenue timing mismatch: PTTEP's upstream EBITDA improves with oil price, but THB bond repayment is fixed. Recommend pre-financing THB bond now while rates remain below 3.15% and before any BOT inflation repricing or THB depreciation (now at 31.58) adds to cost. Mar 3 MAS guidance on Iran sanctions may affect Gulf of Oman JV compliance — complete jurisdictional review in parallel.

Wilmar's USD revolving credit facility should be expanded to accommodate oil-driven input cost inflation. Bunker fuel represents ~12% of cost base — at $79.40 Brent, the annualised fuel cost delta vs Q4 2025 is est. USD 280–320M. Biodiesel mandate tailwind (Indonesia B40, Malaysia B20) improves EBITDA but cash conversion lag creates working capital pressure. Pre-emptive RCF expansion of USD 500–750M recommended before commodity trade finance bank appetite tightens further under Iran secondary sanctions review. iTraxx Asia IG at 132bp is still an executable pricing level.

Energy cost relief window: if Hormuz de-escalates within 4–6 weeks (Base scenario, 42% probability), Brent could retrace toward $68–72 — creating a brief refinancing window for Hyflux's restructuring creditors. Restructuring plan repayment milestones fall due H2 2026; ASEAN HY at 432bp effectively closes the capital markets window for unsecured debt. Explore alternative private credit, DFI, or multilateral bridge financing. Engage restructuring advisor immediately to model refinancing window scenarios contingent on oil trajectory. Energy cost escalation covenant must be reviewed vs restructuring plan assumptions made at $55–60/bbl.
